speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: I think he be transformed into a beast,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: For I can nowhere find him like a man.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: My lord, he is but even now gone hence.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Here was he merry, hearing of a song.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: If he, compact of jars, grow musical,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: We shall have shortly discord in the spheres.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Go seek him. Tell him I would speak with him.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: He saves my labor by his own approach.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Why, how now, monsieur? What a life is this

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: That your poor friends must woo your company?

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: What, you look merrily.

speaker: JAQUES
text: A fool, a fool, I met a fool i’ th’ forest,

speaker: JAQUES
text: A motley fool. A miserable world!

speaker: JAQUES
text: As I do live by food, I met a fool,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Who laid him down and basked him in the sun

speaker: JAQUES
text: And railed on Lady Fortune in good terms,

speaker: JAQUES
text: In good set terms, and yet a motley fool.

speaker: JAQUES
text: “Good morrow, fool,” quoth I. “No, sir,” quoth he,

speaker: JAQUES
text: “Call me not ‘fool’ till heaven hath sent me fortune.”

speaker: JAQUES
text: And then he drew a dial from his poke

speaker: JAQUES
text: And, looking on it with lack-luster eye,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Says very wisely “It is ten o’clock.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Thus we may see,” quoth he, “how the world wags.

speaker: JAQUES
text: ’Tis but an hour ago since it was nine,

speaker: JAQUES
text: And after one hour more ’twill be eleven.

speaker: JAQUES
text: And so from hour to hour we ripe and ripe,

speaker: JAQUES
text: And then from hour to hour we rot and rot,

speaker: JAQUES
text: And thereby hangs a tale.” When I did hear

speaker: JAQUES
text: The motley fool thus moral on the time,

speaker: JAQUES
text: My lungs began to crow like chanticleer

speaker: JAQUES
text: That fools should be so deep-contemplative,

speaker: JAQUES
text: And I did laugh sans intermission

speaker: JAQUES
text: An hour by his dial. O noble fool!

speaker: JAQUES
text: A worthy fool! Motley’s the only wear.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: What fool is this?

speaker: JAQUES
text: O worthy fool!—One that hath been a courtier,

speaker: JAQUES
text: And says “If ladies be but young and fair,

speaker: JAQUES
text: They have the gift to know it.” And in his brain,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Which is as dry as the remainder biscuit

speaker: JAQUES
text: After a voyage, he hath strange places crammed

speaker: JAQUES
text: With observation, the which he vents

speaker: JAQUES
text: In mangled forms. O, that I were a fool!

speaker: JAQUES
text: I am ambitious for a motley coat.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Thou shalt have one.

speaker: JAQUES
text: It is my only suit,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Provided that you weed your better judgments

speaker: JAQUES
text: Of all opinion that grows rank in them

speaker: JAQUES
text: That I am wise. I must have liberty

speaker: JAQUES
text: Withal, as large a charter as the wind,

speaker: JAQUES
text: To blow on whom I please, for so fools have.

speaker: JAQUES
text: And they that are most gallèd with my folly,

speaker: JAQUES
text: They most must laugh. And why, sir, must they so?

speaker: JAQUES
text: The “why” is plain as way to parish church:

speaker: JAQUES
text: He that a fool doth very wisely hit

speaker: JAQUES
text: Doth very foolishly, although he smart,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Not to seem senseless of the bob. If not,

speaker: JAQUES
text: The wise man’s folly is anatomized

speaker: JAQUES
text: Even by the squand’ring glances of the fool.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Invest me in my motley. Give me leave

speaker: JAQUES
text: To speak my mind, and I will through and through

speaker: JAQUES
text: Cleanse the foul body of th’ infected world,

speaker: JAQUES
text: If they will patiently receive my medicine.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Fie on thee! I can tell what thou wouldst do.

speaker: JAQUES
text: What, for a counter, would I do but good?

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Most mischievous foul sin in chiding sin;

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: For thou thyself hast been a libertine,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: As sensual as the brutish sting itself,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And all th’ embossèd sores and headed evils

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: That thou with license of free foot hast caught

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Wouldst thou disgorge into the general world.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Why, who cries out on pride

speaker: JAQUES
text: That can therein tax any private party?

speaker: JAQUES
text: Doth it not flow as hugely as the sea

speaker: JAQUES
text: Till that the weary very means do ebb?

speaker: JAQUES
text: What woman in the city do I name

speaker: JAQUES
text: When that I say the city-woman bears

speaker: JAQUES
text: The cost of princes on unworthy shoulders?

speaker: JAQUES
text: Who can come in and say that I mean her,

speaker: JAQUES
text: When such a one as she such is her neighbor?

speaker: JAQUES
text: Or what is he of basest function

speaker: JAQUES
text: That says his bravery is not on my cost,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Thinking that I mean him, but therein suits

speaker: JAQUES
text: His folly to the mettle of my speech?

speaker: JAQUES
text: There then. How then, what then? Let me see wherein

speaker: JAQUES
text: My tongue hath wronged him. If it do him right,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Then he hath wronged himself. If he be free,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Why then my taxing like a wild goose flies

speaker: JAQUES
text: Unclaimed of any man.

speaker: JAQUES
text: But who comes here?

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Forbear, and eat no more.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Why, I have eat none yet.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Nor shalt not till necessity be served.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Of what kind should this cock come of?

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Art thou thus boldened, man, by thy distress,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Or else a rude despiser of good manners,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: That in civility thou seem’st so empty?

speaker: ORLANDO
text: You touched my vein at first. The thorny point

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Of bare distress hath ta’en from me the show

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Of smooth civility, yet am I inland bred

speaker: ORLANDO
text: And know some nurture. But forbear, I say.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: He dies that touches any of this fruit

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Till I and my affairs are answerèd.

speaker: JAQUES
text: An you will not be answered with reason, I

speaker: JAQUES
text: must die.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: What would you have? Your gentleness shall force

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: More than your force move us to gentleness.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: I almost die for food, and let me have it.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Sit down and feed, and welcome to our table.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Speak you so gently? Pardon me, I pray you.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: I thought that all things had been savage here,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: And therefore put I on the countenance

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Of stern commandment. But whate’er you are

speaker: ORLANDO
text: That in this desert inaccessible,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Under the shade of melancholy boughs,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Lose and neglect the creeping hours of time,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: If ever you have looked on better days,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: If ever been where bells have knolled to church,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: If ever sat at any good man’s feast,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: If ever from your eyelids wiped a tear

speaker: ORLANDO
text: And know what ’tis to pity and be pitied,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Let gentleness my strong enforcement be,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: In the which hope I blush and hide my sword.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: True is it that we have seen better days,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And have with holy bell been knolled to church,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And sat at good men’s feasts and wiped our eyes

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Of drops that sacred pity hath engendered.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And therefore sit you down in gentleness,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And take upon command what help we have

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: That to your wanting may be ministered.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Then but forbear your food a little while

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Whiles, like a doe, I go to find my fawn

speaker: ORLANDO
text: And give it food. There is an old poor man

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Who after me hath many a weary step

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Limped in pure love. Till he be first sufficed,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Oppressed with two weak evils, age and hunger,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: I will not touch a bit.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Go find him out,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And we will nothing waste till you return.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: I thank you; and be blessed for your good comfort.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Thou seest we are not all alone unhappy.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: This wide and universal theater

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Presents more woeful pageants than the scene

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Wherein we play in.

speaker: JAQUES
text: All the world’s a stage,

speaker: JAQUES
text: And all the men and women merely players.

speaker: JAQUES
text: They have their exits and their entrances,

speaker: JAQUES
text: And one man in his time plays many parts,

speaker: JAQUES
text: His acts being seven ages. At first the infant,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Mewling and puking in the nurse’s arms.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Then the whining schoolboy with his satchel

speaker: JAQUES
text: And shining morning face, creeping like snail

speaker: JAQUES
text: Unwillingly to school. And then the lover,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Sighing like furnace, with a woeful ballad

speaker: JAQUES
text: Made to his mistress’ eyebrow. Then a soldier,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Full of strange oaths and bearded like the pard,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Jealous in honor, sudden and quick in quarrel,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Seeking the bubble reputation

speaker: JAQUES
text: Even in the cannon’s mouth. And then the justice,

speaker: JAQUES
text: In fair round belly with good capon lined,

speaker: JAQUES
text: With eyes severe and beard of formal cut,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Full of wise saws and modern instances;

speaker: JAQUES
text: And so he plays his part. The sixth age shifts

speaker: JAQUES
text: Into the lean and slippered pantaloon

speaker: JAQUES
text: With spectacles on nose and pouch on side,

speaker: JAQUES
text: His youthful hose, well saved, a world too wide

speaker: JAQUES
text: For his shrunk shank, and his big manly voice,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Turning again toward childish treble, pipes

speaker: JAQUES
text: And whistles in his sound. Last scene of all,

speaker: JAQUES
text: That ends this strange eventful history,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Is second childishness and mere oblivion,

speaker: JAQUES
text: Sans teeth, sans eyes, sans taste, sans everything.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Welcome. Set down your venerable burden,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And let him feed.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: I thank you most for him.

speaker: ADAM
text: So had you need.—

speaker: ADAM
text: I scarce can speak to thank you for myself.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Welcome. Fall to. I will not trouble you

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: As yet to question you about your fortunes.—

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Give us some music, and, good cousin, sing.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Blow, blow, thou winter wind.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Thou art not so unkind

speaker: AMIENS
text: As man’s ingratitude.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Thy tooth is not so keen,

speaker: AMIENS
text: Because thou art not seen,

speaker: AMIENS
text: Although thy breath be rude.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Heigh-ho, sing heigh-ho, unto the green holly.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Most friendship is feigning, most loving mere folly.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Then heigh-ho, the holly.

speaker: AMIENS
text: This life is most jolly.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Freeze, freeze, thou bitter sky,

speaker: AMIENS
text: That dost not bite so nigh

speaker: AMIENS
text: As benefits forgot.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Though thou the waters warp,

speaker: AMIENS
text: Thy sting is not so sharp

speaker: AMIENS
text: As friend remembered not.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Heigh-ho, sing heigh-ho, unto the green holly.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Most friendship is feigning, most loving mere folly.

speaker: AMIENS
text: Then heigh-ho, the holly.

speaker: AMIENS
text: This life is most jolly.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: If that you were the good Sir Rowland’s son,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: As you have whispered faithfully you were,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And as mine eye doth his effigies witness

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Most truly limned and living in your face,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Be truly welcome hither. I am the duke

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: That loved your father. The residue of your fortune

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Go to my cave and tell me.—Good old man,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Thou art right welcome as thy master is.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text:
To
                 
                Lords
                .
                 
              Support him by the arm.
                 
                To
                 
                Orlando
                .
              Give me your hand,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And let me all your fortunes understand.
